repo.description: Vim-fork focused on extensibility and usability
repo.name: neovim

body:
## Problem:
- We filter progress-events based source. However, it is currently
allowed to be optional. As a result the pattern may match against empty
string.
- progress-message in vim.health doesn't have any source set
- without nested being forced in progress-event progress callbacks don't
work within a autocmd

## Solution:
- Make source mandatory for progress-messages
- Set source of progress-messages in vim.health module to 'vim.health'
- Enable ++nested by default for progress-event

Context: https://github.com/neovim/neovim/pull/38495#issuecomment-4141624142
cc @justinmk

comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -717,8 +717,9 @@ LspProgress                                                       *LspProgress*
         vim.api.nvim_create_autocmd('LspProgress', { buffer = buf, callback = function(ev)
             local value = ev.data.params.value
             vim.api.nvim_echo({ { value.message or 'done' } }, false, {
-              id = 'lsp',
+              id = ev.data.id,
               kind = 'progress',
+              source = 'vim.lsp',

comment_data.body: (I thought we had standardized on `nvim.*` for all namespacing? But ignore me if I'm wrong.)

comment_data.body:
That was my point https://github.com/neovim/neovim/pull/38495#issuecomment-4141424595

But [vim.pack](https://github.com/neovim/neovim/pull/38511) is already being used. And source isn't exactly namespace using module name as source also seems fine to me.

comment_data.body:
Actually from the docs, it seems like `id` only accepts integer that are returned from `nvim_echo` itself.

```
                   • id (`integer|string?`) Message-id returned by a previous
                     `nvim_echo` call, or a user-defined id (string). If
                     existing message has this id, it will be updated instead
                     of creating a new message.
```

         vim.api.nvim_create_autocmd('LspProgress', { buffer = buf, callback = function(ev)

             vim.api.nvim_echo({ { value.message or 'done' } }, false, {
-              id = 'lsp',
+              id = ev.data.id,

comment_data.body:
We can still use strings though.

```lua
vim.api.nvim_create_autocmd('LspProgress', { buffer = buf, callback = function(ev)
    local value = ev.data.params.value
    vim.api.nvim_echo({ { value.message or 'done' } }, false, {
      id = string.format("lsp: %d", ev.data.client_id),
      kind = 'progress',
      source = 'vim.lsp',
      title = value.title,
      status = value.kind ~= 'end' and 'running' or 'success',
      percent = value.percentage,
    })
  end,
})
```

comment_regular_data.body: This needs to include a corresponding update to the `:h LspProgress` snippet (see https://github.com/neovim/neovim/pull/38495#issuecomment-4143985320).

We could relax this later. Either by generating a `source` from the stacktrace or some other context. Or later, by leveraging "plugin identity": 

- https://github.com/neovim/neovim/issues/34704

but for now, it's important that the progress "owner" gives a hint.
I've added update to `h LspProgress` doc and have set ++nested for progress-events as extra.

title: fix(progress): make source mandatory for progress-message
